confidence_rationale: This video is a research presentation (SAGES 2011) comparing laparoscopic skills performance across single-site access devices vs. independent port setups. It contains no actual performance footage of peg transfer or any other FLS task. The content consists entirely of slides with study methodology, results graphs, and conclusions. No trainee or expert performer is visible performing the task.
cannot_determine: Completion time — no performance footage present
Phase timings — no task execution visible
Task completion — only research data presentation shown

teaching_content.study_focus: Comparison of laparoscopic skills performance using single-site access (SSA) devices (GelPOINT, SILS Port, SSL Access System, TriPort) versus independent-port SSA setup across peg transfer, bean drop, pattern cutting, and extracorporeal suturing tasks. Participants: 14 medical students, 4 residents, 5 attending surgeons.
key_findings_presented: Attending surgeons significantly faster than residents or students (p < 0.001)
SSA devices associated with 100+ seconds longer total times vs. independent port setup for residents and surgeons (differences not statistically significant)
Task performance not significantly different across different SSA devices
Independent-port SSA faster than most device-based SSA setups for peg transfer (3/4 devices), pattern cutting (3/4 devices), and total time (4/4 devices)
Independent-port SSA showed no advantage for bean drop (0/4 devices) or suturing (2/4 devices)

summary: This is a research conference presentation, not a performance video. No FLS task execution is visible. Scoring is not applicable. The presentation documents a comparative study of laparoscopic task performance using different single-site access platforms in a trainer box model.
